To: Marcus Lindqvist
From: PC
Subject: Federation — harness slip + CR-204, let's reconcile before SC

Marcus — two things, and I'd rather work both through directly with you before the SC next week.

1) The test harness. It was due Friday and didn't land, and we didn't get a heads-up — I went looking for it. I'm flagging it plainly because it's the second slip in six weeks and this one sits on the critical path: the conformance re-test can't start without it. I'm not looking to make a drama of it — your team did genuinely good work on the broker build and I've said so to R. What I need is two things: your read on what happened, and an agreement that anything at risk of slipping gets a flag from your side before the due date, not after. If we have that, I can manage the rest around it.

2) CR-204. I've read it against the SOW and the integration scope it describes reads to me as already covered under section 4.2 — so as written I don't think it's additional, and I don't want to send an in-scope change to approvals. I know your account manager raised it and cc'd the sponsor's office; I'd suggest you and I reconcile the CR line items against 4.2 first, between us, and then put a corrected position to the sponsor jointly rather than have it argued out in the SC. If, going through it, there's genuinely new scope in there, I'll back the delta — I just want it to be the actual delta.

For the record I'm logging both items with specifics (harness due-date and the missed flag; CR-204 vs SOW 4.2) on the issues log so we're working off the same facts, not so anyone's building a case.

Can we take 30-45 minutes tomorrow, just the two of us, before this hardens? I'd like us to walk into the SC aligned.

PC

Rationale: hold Meridian to the contract firmly but as a partner — name the miss and get a heads-up protocol, treat the CR as a scope reconciliation against the SOW, keep it between counterparts and on the record, and reserve any formal escalation for if this doesn't resolve.
